title: Erdős 突破：OpenAI 模型首次实现重大 AI 数学发现
author: OpenAI
content:
The Erdős Breakthrough: OpenAI Models Achieve First Major AI Mathematical Discovery

For the first time, artificial intelligence has crossed the boundary from verifying existing mathematics to generating entirely new, foundational scientific breakthroughs. In a recent video update from OpenAI, researchers shared details of an unexpected milestone: an AI model has successfully solved a famous, long-standing open problem in combinatorial geometry.

The achievement marks a critical turning point in computer science. While AI has previously been used to assist in calculations or automate routine elements of data analysis, this event represents the first clear instance of an artificial intelligence resolving a major, widely recognized unsolved mathematical problem.

Solving the Intricate Geometry of Points in a Plane

The specific challenge conquered by the AI focuses on a problem involving the configuration of points in a plane. Described by researchers as an elementary geometric problem in combinatorial geometry, the task appears straightforward superficially but possesses immense complexity beneath the surface.

Prior to this result, human mathematicians believed that the existing geometric constructions for this problem were optimal and essentially "best possible." However, the OpenAI model discovered a way to significantly improve upon these historical benchmarks.

Overcoming Human Limitations

According to the engineering team, human researchers were previously unable to execute this specific proof path due to its incredibly delicate nature. The process required making thousands of hyper-specific tactical choices along the way, creating an overwhelming matrix of decisions that humans could not navigate comprehensively.

To bridge this gap, the AI model leveraged deep structural tools from algebraic number theory. It systematically explored the dense web of proof possibilities far more exhaustively than any human mathematician could, ultimately charting an entirely new path to the solution.

Initial Disbelief and Shortened Timelines

The sudden appearance of the solution caught the OpenAI research team entirely off guard. Several team members recollected their initial shock, admitting they spent days reading over the model's outputs to rule out errors.

"I remember seeing an initial version of the model output, I sort of didn't really believe it... I was like, you cannot be serious. I mean, this sounds like too good to be true."

Beyond the immediate mathematical victory, this milestone has major implications for the field of artificial general intelligence. Researchers noted that while they always expected AI systems to eventually achieve autonomous scientific discovery, this singular result has dramatically shortened their internal development timelines.

Dawn of a Golden Era for Science

The successful resolution of an open combinatorial geometry problem serves as an early indicator of what OpenAI describes as a looming "golden era for mathematics." Crucially, the underlying capabilities that allowed the model to navigate this proof are not limited strictly to pure math.

The team emphasizes that because the AI has demonstrated an ability to map complex systems and uncover novel paths to truth, these same systems can be deployed across a wide array of scientific disciplines. Looking forward, OpenAI anticipates that these models will soon drive similar foundational breakthroughs in:

  • Engineering: Optimizing complex structures and mechanical design paths.
  • Physics: Navigating multi-variable simulations and quantum theory frameworks.
  • Biology & Medicine: Mapping cellular behaviors, genetic sequences, and novel drug formulations.

Ultimately, this achievement will likely be viewed historically as the moment AI transitioned from an assistive software utility into an active, collaborative engine for global scientific progress.
aiSummary: OpenAI 研究人员宣布 AI 首次取得重大数学发现：AI 模型解决了组合几何中著名的 Erdős 单位距离问题，超越了此前被认为最优的构造方案。
oneSentenceSummary: OpenAI 研究人员宣布 AI 首次取得重大数学发现：AI 模型解决了组合几何中著名的 Erdős 单位距离问题，超越了此前被认为最优的构造方案。
summary: OpenAI 研究人员以亲历者视角分享了这一历史性 AI 成就：AI 模型首次解决了组合几何领域一个久负盛名的开放问题，这也是该数学子领域最广为人知的未解问题之一。该问题涉及平面上点的排列（即 Erdős 单位距离问题），表面上是纯粹的初等几何题，但其解法却需要代数数论中的深层工具。在此结果出现之前，已有的几何构造方案被普遍认为已接近最优。AI 模型证明，这一构造实际上还可以得到显著改进。关键洞察在于：证明路径需要做出海量极为精密的连续决策，其复杂程度超出了人类数学家系统执行的能力范畴；而 AI 得以穷举探索全部证明可能性，并最终找到一条可行路径。研究人员坦言，看到结果的第一反应是难以置信，这让他们辗转难眠，因为结论「好得不像真的」。除数学成就本身之外，这一里程碑也大幅压缩了团队对 AI 能力发展的内部时间线预期。团队强调，支撑这项发现的底层推理能力并不局限于纯数学，同样的系统预计将在工程、物理、生物和医学领域推动基础性突破。研究人员将此视为数学与更广泛科学发现黄金时代的开端。

mainPoints.point: AI 首次解决了组合几何领域最广为人知的开放问题之一。
mainPoints.explanation: AI 模型攻克了 Erdős 单位距离问题——该问题长期悬而未决，已有构造方案被认为已达最优。这是 AI 首次明确解决一个著名、广受关注的未解数学问题，而非某个冷僻的小众难题。

mainPoints.point: 证明需要代数数论的深层工具，以及人类无法完成的数百万次精密连续决策。
mainPoints.explanation: 人类数学家虽了解大致的证明思路，却无力付诸实施：沿证明路径所需做出的精确连续选择数量极为庞大，已超出人类系统执行的能力边界。AI 得以穷举探索所有可能性，最终找到了可行路径。

mainPoints.point: 这一结果大幅压缩了研究人员对 AI 科学发现的内部时间线预期。
mainPoints.explanation: 研究人员虽预期 AI 迟早能实现自主科学突破，但这一结果来得远比预想早得多。多位团队成员表示，他们的时间线被显著压缩，凸显了 AI 能力发展的加速态势。

mainPoints.point: 这一突破揭示了 AI 推动全科学领域基础性进展的潜力。
mainPoints.explanation: OpenAI 研究人员强调，支撑这次数学突破的推理能力具有跨领域的普适性。同样的系统预计将在工程、物理、生物和医学领域产生类似的基础性发现，而不仅限于数学。

keyQuotes: 我认为这一时刻的重要性在于：这是 AI 首次明确解决的不仅是一个未解数学问题，而是一个真正广为人知的未解数学问题。
这道题涉及平面上的点，是一个完全初等的几何问题，但其解法却需要代数数论中的深层工具。此前人们认为已有的构造基本上已是最优解，而我们的模型证明这一构造实际上还可以得到相当大幅度的改进。
人类无法执行这一方法，因为它实在太精细，证明过程中需要做出的决策太多了。AI 得以更全面地探索所有这些可能性，并从中找到了一条路，找到了通往解答的途径。
我预期 AI 迟早会做到这样的事，但这个结果让我的时间线大幅提前了。
这个结果让我看到了数学黄金时代的曙光。我认为这是一次质的飞跃，回望历史，它将被视为数学史上真正重要的时刻。
